name: kingdoms
description: Imperial and colonial territories per decade, 1650-1730. 'from'/'to' are inclusive decade windows. Coordinates [lon,lat] WGS84. Coastal shapes are coarse but recognizable; interior borders schematic.

properties.label: Spain
properties.note: Habsburg Spain to 1700, Bourbon Philip V after; Gibraltar and Minorca lost 1704-13 (Parry, The Spanish Seaborne Empire).

properties.label: New Spain
properties.note: Viceroyalty of New Spain: Mexico, Central America, the Manila galleon terminus at Acapulco; silver of Zacatecas and Potosi de los Reyes.

properties.label: Spanish Florida
properties.note: St Augustine and the mission provinces; raided by Carolina (1702-04) and by pirates; the 1715 plate-fleet wrecks lie off its east coast.

properties.label: Tierra Firme & Peru
properties.note: The Spanish Main, New Granada, Peru and Chile; treasure flows Potosi-Arica-Callao-Panama (Parry). Interior limits schematic.

properties.label: Cuba
properties.note: Havana, key of the Indies, where the combined flotas assemble for the homeward run through the Florida Strait.

properties.label: Hispaniola
properties.note: Nominally all Spanish, but the west and Tortuga are already slipping to French and English buccaneers (Cordingly).

properties.label: Santo Domingo
properties.note: Eastern Hispaniola; the western third lost de facto to French buccaneers, formally ceded at Ryswick 1697.

properties.label: Puerto Rico
properties.note: San Juan's Morro guards the Windward route into the Caribbean; repulsed every assault in this period.

properties.label: Jamaica (Spanish)
properties.note: Thinly held Spanish island; seized by Penn and Venables' Western Design expedition, 1655.

properties.label: Philippines
properties.note: Manila and the galleon trade to Acapulco; Spanish control thin south of the Visayas, where Sulu and Maguindanao hold sway.

properties.label: England, Scotland & Ireland
properties.note: Commonwealth then Restoration kingdoms; the Navigation Acts (1651, 1660) bind the colonies to English shipping.

properties.label: Great Britain & Ireland
properties.note: United as Great Britain by the Act of Union 1707; by 1730 the leading naval power (Rodger, The Command of the Ocean).

properties.label: New England
properties.note: Massachusetts, Connecticut, Rhode Island, New Hampshire; Boston the chief port and shipbuilder of English America.

properties.label: Virginia & Maryland
properties.note: The tobacco Chesapeake; annual tobacco convoys home are prime privateer and pirate prey.

properties.label: Carolina
properties.note: Charles Town founded 1670 by Barbadian planters; rice and deerskins; blockaded by Blackbeard 1718.

properties.label: New York & the Jerseys
properties.note: New Netherland taken 1664, briefly Dutch again 1673-74, English by Westminster 1674; New York money quietly backed the 1690s Pirate Round.

properties.label: Pennsylvania
properties.note: Penn's proprietary colony from 1681; Philadelphia soon among the busiest American ports.

properties.label: Jamaica
properties.note: Taken from Spain 1655; Port Royal the buccaneer capital until the 1692 earthquake, thereafter sugar and the Royal Navy's Jamaica Station.

properties.label: Barbados & the Leeward Islands
properties.note: Barbados, St Kitts, Nevis, Antigua, Montserrat: the sugar revolution's first home; St Kitts shared with France until 1713.

properties.label: Bermuda
properties.note: Settled 1612; its sloops and salt-rakers range the whole western Atlantic.

properties.label: Bahamas
properties.note: English from Eleuthera 1648; ungoverned pirate nest 1706-18 until Woodes Rogers restores crown rule at Nassau.

properties.label: Hudson Bay (Rupert's Land)
properties.note: Hudson's Bay Company charter 1670; forts traded with France until Utrecht 1713 confirmed British possession.

properties.label: Nova Scotia
properties.note: Acadia taken at Port Royal 1710, ceded at Utrecht 1713; renamed Nova Scotia, though Acadian settlers remain.

properties.label: Newfoundland (English Shore)
properties.note: The cod fishery of the Avalon; French Plaisance surrendered at Utrecht 1713; sacked by pirates Easton to Roberts.

properties.label: Gibraltar
properties.note: Taken by Rooke's fleet 1704, confirmed at Utrecht 1713; the Mediterranean squadron's watch on the Strait.

properties.label: Bombay
properties.note: Ceded by Portugal in Catherine of Braganza's dowry 1661, leased to the East India Company 1668; besieged in the Mughal war of 1689-90.

properties.label: Madras (Fort St George)
properties.note: East India Company factory from 1639; the Coromandel cloth trade, prey and market alike for Indian Ocean pirates.

properties.label: English Gold Coast forts
properties.note: Cape Coast Castle (taken 1664) and lesser forts of the Royal African Company; gold, then slaves.

properties.label: France
properties.note: Louis XIV's kingdom; Colbert builds the navy from the 1660s, war finance erodes it after 1694 toward guerre de course.

properties.label: New France (Canada)
properties.note: The St Lawrence colony and fur-trade hinterland; Quebec repulsed Phips 1690 and Walker 1711.

properties.label: Louisiana
properties.note: Claimed by La Salle 1682, settled from Biloxi 1699; New Orleans founded 1718. Borders entirely schematic.

properties.label: Acadia
properties.note: French Acadia, taken and retaken; finally lost with Port Royal 1710 and ceded at Utrecht 1713.

properties.label: Île Royale (Cape Breton)
properties.note: Retained at Utrecht; the fortress of Louisbourg rises from 1713 to guard the Gulf fishery.

properties.label: Tortuga & the Coast of Saint-Domingue
properties.note: Buccaneer and boucanier country under nominal French governors (d'Ogeron from 1665); de facto French long before Ryswick.

properties.label: Saint-Domingue
properties.note: Formally ceded at Ryswick 1697; sugar boom makes it the richest colony in the Americas by mid-century.

properties.label: Martinique & Guadeloupe
properties.note: French since 1635; Martinique the seat of the French Windward Islands government and privateer base in wartime.

properties.label: Cayenne
properties.note: Definitively French from 1664; a small plantation colony on the wild Guiana coast.

properties.label: Pondicherry
properties.note: Compagnie des Indes factory founded 1674; held by the Dutch 1693-99, returned at Ryswick.

properties.label: Île Bourbon
properties.note: Settled 1665 as a way-station to the Indies; in the 1720s its governors quietly pardoned and absorbed Indian Ocean pirates.

properties.label: Portugal
properties.note: Independent of Spain again from 1640 (recognized 1668); Lisbon the hub of the Brazil fleets and, after 1697, Brazilian gold.

properties.label: Brazil
properties.note: Dutch Pernambuco expelled 1654; sugar, then the Minas Gerais gold rush after 1695 (Boxer, The Golden Age of Brazil).

properties.label: Colônia do Sacramento
properties.note: Founded 1680 opposite Buenos Aires as a smuggling door into Spanish Plate; taken and restored repeatedly.

properties.label: Angola
properties.note: Luanda and Benguela, retaken from the Dutch 1648; the chief slaving coast for Brazil.

properties.label: Mozambique & Sofala
properties.note: Mozambique Island and the Zambezi prazos; way-station of the Carreira da Índia.

properties.label: Goa
properties.note: Seat of the Estado da Índia, much diminished by Dutch conquests but still the Rome of the East.

properties.label: Macau
properties.note: Tolerated by the Ming and then the Qing; the only European foothold on the China coast.

properties.label: Cape Verde Islands
properties.note: Victualling and slaving entrepôt on the Guinea run; frequently plundered by passing pirates.

properties.label: United Provinces
properties.note: The Dutch Republic; VOC and WIC chartered monopolies carry its flag worldwide (Boxer, The Dutch Seaborne Empire).

properties.label: New Netherland
properties.note: New Amsterdam and the Hudson fur trade; surrendered to Nicolls 1664, briefly retaken 1673-74.

properties.label: Essequibo & Berbice
properties.note: WIC trading colonies on the wild coast of Guiana.

properties.label: Dutch Guiana & Suriname
properties.note: Suriname taken from England 1667 and kept at Breda in exchange for New Netherland.

properties.label: Curaçao & St Eustatius
properties.note: Free-trading entrepôts inside the Spanish Main; Curaçao a major slave depot, Statia the smugglers' golden rock.

properties.label: Cape Colony
properties.note: VOC refreshment station founded 1652; the tavern of two oceans on the Indies run.

properties.label: Dutch Ceylon (coastal belt)
properties.note: Colombo 1656, Jaffna 1658: cinnamon coasts held by the VOC; the kingdom of Kandy keeps the interior.

properties.label: Dutch Malabar (Cochin)
properties.note: Cochin taken from Portugal 1663; pepper forts along the Malabar coast.

properties.label: Batavia & the Java coast
properties.note: Batavia the VOC capital of Asia; north-coast ports under Company sway, Mataram inland.

properties.label: Spice Islands (Moluccas & Banda)
properties.note: Clove and nutmeg monopoly enforced by the VOC at Ambon, Banda, Ternate and Tidore.

properties.label: Malacca
properties.note: Taken from Portugal 1641; the VOC's lock on the Strait of Malacca.

properties.label: Dutch Gold Coast (Elmina)
properties.note: Elmina, taken from Portugal 1637; WIC headquarters for the Guinea gold and slave trades.

properties.label: Dutch Formosa
properties.note: Fort Zeelandia on Taiwan; lost to Koxinga's Ming-loyalist fleet in 1662.

properties.label: Kingdom of Tungning (Taiwan)
properties.note: Koxinga and his heirs rule Taiwan 1662-83, raiding the Qing coast until Shi Lang's conquest.

properties.label: Ottoman Empire
properties.note: At greatest extent: Hungary, Podolia (1672), Crete won 1669; checked at Vienna 1683.

properties.label: Ottoman Empire
properties.note: After Karlowitz 1699: Hungary and Podolia lost, the Morea lost 1699 and regained 1715; Banat ceded at Passarowitz 1718.

properties.label: Barbary Regencies
properties.note: Algiers, Tunis and Tripoli: Ottoman in name, corsair republics in practice; treaties with England (1682) and others slowly tame them.

properties.label: Morocco & Salé
properties.note: The Salé rovers' republic absorbed by the Alaouite sultans; Moulay Ismail (1672-1727) ransoms Christian captives by the thousand.

properties.label: Mughal Empire
properties.note: Shah Jahan and early Aurangzeb: Hindustan from Kabul to Bengal; Bijapur and Golconda still independent in the Deccan.

properties.label: Mughal Empire (Aurangzeb's peak)
properties.note: Bijapur (1686) and Golconda (1687) annexed; the empire claims nearly all India, but Maratha war drains it.

properties.label: Mughal Empire (declining)
properties.note: After Aurangzeb's death 1707 the Marathas and provincial nawabs hollow the empire; Delhi sacked by Nadir Shah 1739.

properties.label: Safavid Persia
properties.note: Safavid shahs hold the Gulf shore from Basra's edge to Makran; Bandar Abbas the silk and spice outlet, with English and Dutch factories.

properties.label: Persia (in turmoil)
properties.note: Afghan rebels take Isfahan 1722; Russians and Ottomans bite at the Caspian fringes until Nadir restores order in the 1730s.

properties.label: Qing China
properties.note: The Manchu conquest completes in the south by 1683; the coast sealed against Koxinga 1661-83, then reopened to trade at Canton and Amoy.

properties.label: Taiwan (Qing)
properties.note: Admiral Shi Lang destroys the Zheng fleet at Penghu, 1683; Taiwan annexed to Fujian province.

properties.label: Tokugawa Japan
properties.note: Closed under the sakoku edicts; only the Dutch at Deshima and Chinese junks trade at Nagasaki.

properties.label: Russia (Siberia)
properties.note: Cossack fur frontier reaches the Pacific by 1650, Kamchatka by 1697; Bering sails for the strait 1728. Entirely schematic.

properties.label: Danish St Thomas
properties.note: Danish West India Company colony from 1672; a neutral, ask-no-questions market that pirates and smugglers prize.

properties.label: Danish Gold Coast (Christiansborg)
properties.note: Fort Christiansborg at Accra, Danish from the 1660s, feeding the St Thomas slave market.

properties.label: Swedish Empire (Baltic)
properties.note: Dominium maris baltici: Finland, Estonia, Livonia, Ingria; broken in the Great Northern War (Glete, Navies and Nations).

properties.label: Sweden
properties.note: After Nystad 1721 the Baltic provinces pass to Russia; Sweden keeps Finland but the empire is over.
